description: Create a public subnet with two options: let Archie create a new VPC for you, or add a subnet to your existing VPC. Perfect for adding public-facing resources like web servers, load balancers, or bastion hosts.
architecture_principles: Flexible VPC Options: Create new VPC or use existing - Archie handles dependencies automatically
VPC Scanning: Automatically discover existing VPCs in your account for easy selection
Internet Access: Automatic Internet Gateway creation and routing for public connectivity
Smart Defaults: Pre-configured CIDR blocks that avoid conflicts
Quick Setup: Minimal configuration required - Archie takes care of the complexity

allowed_values.label: Create New VPC
allowed_values.description: Let Archie create a new VPC with optimal settings

allowed_values.label: Use Existing VPC
allowed_values.description: Add subnet to an existing VPC in your account

vpcMode.help_text: Choose whether to create a new VPC or use an existing one

vpcName.description: Name for the new VPC (only for 'new' mode)
vpcName.help_text: Only required when creating a new VPC

vpcCidr.description: CIDR block for the new VPC (only for 'new' mode)
vpcCidr.help_text: Auto-generated unique CIDR if not specified

vpcId.help_text: Scan your account or enter VPC ID manually

allowed_values.label: Auto Calculate
allowed_values.description: Automatically calculate CIDR based on VPC configuration

cidrBlock.help_text: Must be within your VPC CIDR range. Each /24 provides 251 usable IP addresses

availabilityZone.help_text: Leave empty for automatic selection, or choose a specific AZ for precise placement

mapPublicIp.description: Automatically assign public IP addresses to instances launched in this subnet
mapPublicIp.help_text: Recommended for public subnets. Instances will be directly accessible from the internet

createIgw.description: Create and attach an Internet Gateway for internet access
createIgw.help_text: Required for internet connectivity. Disable only if your VPC already has an IGW

outputs.subnet_id: The ID of the created subnet (use this for launching instances)
outputs.availability_zone: The availability zone where subnet was created
outputs.cidr_block: The CIDR block of the subnet
outputs.route_table_id: Route table ID associated with the subnet
